message:
fix(term,client): reclaim VecDeque capacity after scroll-off, fix subscriber leak

Two memory management fixes for long-lived mux server sessions:

1. screen.rs: VecDeque<Line> never called shrink_to_fit() during
   normal scroll-off operations (only during explicit erase_scrollback).
   Over 23 days with 50+ panes, the ring buffer retains peak capacity
   indefinitely. Fix: after bulk line removal in scroll_down, shrink
   when capacity exceeds 2x actual length (threshold: >1024 entries).
   This balances memory reclamation against reallocation cost.

2. client/domain.rs: ClientDomain::new() called Mux::subscribe() but
   discarded the sub_id. Each domain attach/detach cycle leaked one
   closure in the Mux::subscribers HashMap. Fix: store sub_id in the
   struct and implement Drop to call Mux::unsubscribe().

Both fixes address root causes of the observed 76GB RSS after 23 days
of agent swarm operation (wa-3kxe.1 bead).

Validated: 261 term tests, 10 client tests, 304 mux tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
